comment_content:
United fans that complaining about the manager need to shut up about the system.. the fact that you haven’t figure out that 3-4-3 is just the structure and the system is flexible where they switching to 4-4-2 or 3-1-5-1 when they attacking and 4-4-2- or 5-3-2 when defending during the game tells the system is unpredictable .. when the team is flowing into the system they look unstoppable because the other team constantly trying to do adjustments while Man U switching the formation.. The reason the team can’t play the system consistently is due to lack of quality within the squad and most players aren’t even build to play this system. The coach is managing with what he has available. 
For instant ARSENAL is only consistent this season because they literally have 2 teams. They have at least 2 top players for each position and that brings competition between the players to perform at their best. United doesn’t even have top starting 11. Can you see how far you still from wining the league or play consistent football? Guess who knows this.. AMORIM.. his situation at Sporting was easier because they gave him full control and got rid of at least 12 players in the first season and by second season he had literally new squad and he won the league. As Sporting FC fan I fell sorry for Amorim because he’s dealing with spoiled  fans that never seen a process of building a team as most of you only seen Fergie with already stabilised and running the club on winning streaks.

comment_content: We are desperate for a real captain and some proper leadership! I have never known a captain who gives the ball away so often and gets on his teammates case when he over hits a pass or cross or gives away possession!   Also, if one of our big summer signings is having a nightmare it's OK to take him off earlier in the game and tell him maybe next week son! Also, this subbing of CB's every game has to stop!!! Martinez just switched off for their last goal and did exactly the same thing a few minutes later but Senne spared his/our blushes! I love Martinez but he is obviously not back to himself yet so why bring him on when we are chasing the game? We are the only team who subs CB's week in week out and it hinders us! OT is a huge pitch and we have no holding midfielders on the field, week in week out we get outrun in midfield at Old Trafford and our opposition runs through the middle of the park creating chance after chance, but the manager won't change the system/personnel on the pitch!  Insanity Is Doing the Same Thing Over and Over Again and Expecting Different Results!!!

comment_content:
Man United’s second and fourth goals conceded came from the same scenario: being exposed straight through the middle, right in front of the defensive line. This has been a recurring pattern since Ruben Amorim arrived at Man Utd. His rigid commitment to the 3-4-2-1 system makes the team defensively passive: the back line is overloaded but largely irrelevant, while the true battleground: the midfield is constantly understaffed. Once the first line of pressing is beaten, the midfield is easily overrun by the opponent’s numbers.

Beyond the midfield problem, a core weakness of any three-centre-back system lies on the flanks, as it relies heavily on the wing-backs’ all-round ability. With the two attacking midfielders naturally drifting inside to operate behind or rotate with the centre-forward, the wide areas are often outnumbered, leaving only one wing-back per side. When opponents attack quickly down the wings, wing-backs are frequently forced into 1v2 situations, as attacking midfielders' trackback support arrives late due to diagonal recovery runs.

On paper, the 3-4-2-1 may appear balanced, but in reality it is deeply unbalanced. It usually works only for underdog teams prioritising deep defence and quick counter-attacks. Consistently dominating opponents with this system is extremely difficult, unless you have an elite ball-winning midfielder like peak N’Golo Kante and Nemanja Matic, along with exceptionally athletic wing-backs.

There are rumours that Amorim wants to sign Adam Wharton, Carlos Baleba, Morten Hjulmand, and even Conor Gallagher, while Kobbie Mainoo remains on the bench. This is clearly not the way forward imo.

comment_content:
Somebody should tell the manager to stop playing Bruno out of position! 

EVERY attack-minded & skillful player loses possession & will give away the ball. The difference is that they do it at the top-end of the pitch, further away from goal so that the Defenders are not under immediate pressure  bcos there would still be CM/DM players behind him to try and recover the ball. 


DeBruyne, Ronaldinho, Modric, Seedorf, Juninho, Odegaard, Kroos, Bellingham, Iniesta, Xavi, Foden, Muller, Kaka, Sneijder, Zidane, Deco, David+Bernardo Silva, etc. all lose possession and give away the ball multiple times in a match...but their coaches don't play them in DM roles bcos that is NOT who they are.

Playing your most creative player (G/A) in a DM position does NOT make any sense whatsoever bcos you deprive your attack the full potential of his creativity & input...and you are making your DM/defense vulnerable & under undue pressure. 

Trying to fit square pegs in round holes is your fault, and not the fault of the pegs nor the holes!
